rulings.Case Title: POODLES, INC., VS. ROGER C. KUHN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' All Animals Properties, Lp, 1333 Ninth Avenue Llc, And All Animals Emergency Hospital, Inc'S Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 9, 2019 line 2. Defendants' All Animals Properties, LP, 1333 Ninth Avenue LLC, And All Animals Emergency Hospital, Inc'S Motion For Protective Order GRANTED in part. Deposition of Mr. Perdue is to proceed with the following conditions: 1) deposition to be on topics and events which predate this litigation; 2) deposition may inquire into facts which are presented in any declaration Mr. Perdue files; and 3) deposition shall be calendared after the deadline for filing a motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication. Issues relating to Mr. Perdue's trial testimony, if any, are not resolved by this motion. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KEYHAN MOHANNA ET AL VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 9, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT: Defendant Wells Fargo's Demurrer to SAC is SUSTAINED, without leave to amend as to the following causes of action: 1) Negligence, 2) Bad Faith Breach of Good Faith, 3) violation of Unfair Business Practices §17200, 4) Constructive Fraud, 5) Conspiracy to Defraud, 6) Trespass, 7) Assault, 8) Battery, 9) Elder Abuse, and 10) Wrongful Foreclosure. Plaintiffs failed to follow Court's instructions per November 14, 2018 order. No opposition filed. Defendant Wells Fargo's Demurrer to SAC is SUSTAINED, with final leave to amend the following two causes of action. Per the IIED claim, Plaintiff shall remove allegations regarding events outside SOL period which are time-barred under CCP 335.1. He must specifically allege outrageous conduct perpetrated by each defendant and when such alleged conduct took place. If SOL was suspended, Plaintiff to provide supporting allegations. Per the intentional interference with prospective economic advantage claim, Plaintiffs must explain how Wells Fargo's alleged acts interfered with their business relationship and explain exactly how they were harmed. If wrongful acts based on fraud, allegations must factually specify individuals, dates, and amounts lost. No Opposition filed. Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED as to Exs. 23 and 24. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KEYHAN MOHANNA ET AL VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 9, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT DUKE PARTNERS II LLC's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT: Defendant Duke Partners' Demurrer to SAC is SUSTAINED, without leave to amend as to the following causes of action: 1) Negligence, 2) violation of Unfair Business Practices §17200, 3) Conspiracy to Defraud, 4) Wrongful Foreclosure, and 5) Wrongful Eviction. Plaintiffs failed to follow Court's instructions per November 14, 2018 order. No opposition filed. Defendant Duke Partners' Demurrer to SAC is SUSTAINED, with final leave to amend the following three causes of action. Per the IIED claim, Plaintiff shall remove allegations regarding events outside SOL period which are time-barred under CCP 335.1. He must specifically allege what outrageous conduct was perpetrated by each defendant and when such alleged conduct took place. If SOL was suspended, Plaintiff to provide supporting allegations. Per the intentional interference with prospective economic advantage claim, Plaintiffs must explain how Duke Partners' alleged acts interfered with their business relationship and explain exactly how they were harmed. If wrongful acts based on fraud, allegations must be factually specify individuals, dates, and amounts lost. Per Quiet Title claim, P must provide a legal description of Unit 4 under CCP § 761.020, and it should also specifically provide allegations supporting 3H's equitable ownership, notwithstanding foreclosure sale to Duke. The exhibits provided in the complaint and those offered for judicial notice controvert Ps' allegation that loans connected with Unit 4 were forgiven. No Opposition filed. Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED as to Exs. H, I, and J. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN MANHAN ET AL VS. SHANNON GALLAGHER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Or To Set Aside Sanction Orders Or To Rehear Plaintiffs' Motions For Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative For Excusable Neglect
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 9, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT SHANNON GALLAGHER's Motion For Reconsideration Or To Set Aside Sanction Orders Or To Rehear Plaintiffs' Motions For Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative For Excusable Neglect continued to May 23, 2019. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of the opposition to Department 501 no later than May 13, 2019 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PHYLLIS SPARKS-MAGDALUYO VS. NEW PENN FINANCIAL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 9, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANTs NEW PENN FINANCIAL, LLC, and THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Plaintiff's first, second, third, fourth, and fifth causes of action state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against Defendants; Plaintiff has standing under the first cause of action (Yvanova v. New Century Mortgage Corp. (2016) 62 Cal.4th 919, 939, 942-43); Plaintiff has sufficiently pled causation for UCL standing in their third cause of action. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MOAB INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. COURTHOUSE VENTURES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Appoint Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 9, 2019 line 6. PLAINTIFF MOAB INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC's Motion To Appoint Receiver CONTINUED to May 21, 2019. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1300(c). Proof of service on all active parties must be filed no later than May 14, 2019, at least 5 court days before the hearing. There is no proof of service on file for any of the moving papers, including the reply. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KEYHAN MOHANNA VS. PAMELA JACKSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To 1)Deem Plaintiff Keyhan Mohanna A Vexatious Litigant, 2)Enter A Pre-Filing Order Pursuant To C.C.P. 391.7, And 3)Dismiss The Present Case And-Or Order That Security Be Posted For Benefit Of De
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 9, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT PAMELA JACKSON's Motion To 1)Deem Plaintiff Keyhan Mohanna A Vexatious Litigant, 2)Enter A Pre-Filing Order Pursuant To C.C.P. 391.7, And 3)Dismiss The Present Case And-Or Order That Security Be Posted For Benefit Of Defendant DENIED. The Court maintains jurisdiction to rule on the vexatious litigant motion despite the dismissal of the case per Pittman v. Beck Park Apartments Ltd. (2018) 20 Cal.App. 5th 1009, 1021. The Court notes that in at least two prior law suits, Vivien Mohanna has appeared in pro per with power of attorney in contravention of law. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KI CHI LEE VS. SHIE CHUNG MOU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 9, 2019 line 9. DEFENDANTs SHIE MOU, BING WANG, QIURONG MAIO, and WEIBIN WANG's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer against each defendant and is not ambiguous or unintelligible. Allegations in the complaint, as well as exhibits provided, are taken as true for the purposes of ruling on the demurrer. = (501/CFH)
